title: Hash Tables, Dictionaries, Sets, and Collision Policy · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Create a deduplication and lookup service that remains correct under collision-heavy data. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a hash-table model and workload benchmark. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
Trace separate chaining and open addressing through collision and deletion cases.
Explain how load factor and resizing influence expected cost and memory.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
